title: Default and Direct Exchanges, Work Queues, and Fair Dispatch · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first direct-routing work distribution service for default and direct exchanges, work queues, and fair dispatch. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of direct-routing work distribution service while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.
Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve.
Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Default Exchange Coupling
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 5189 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Worker Effect Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Worker Effect Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop messaging team handles case 7138 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Worker Effect Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Worker Effect Evidence, keep Worker Effect Evidence connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.
